blocks.text: DTC P062F: ECU internal circuit error (EEPROM)

The PGM-FI ECU has a built-in EEPROM. Information that needs to be protected for powertrain control (control learning values, etc.) and information about the vehicle (frame numbers, etc.) are stored in the EEPROM so that the information can be retained even when power is not supplied to the PGM-FI ECU, such as when the 12V battery is disconnected. If the data reading process does not end normally, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the EEPROM has failed and stores the DTC. The CPU also writes data from the CPU to the EEPROM at the timing requested for each retained data (for control-related information, when the ignition is turned on mode, and for vehicle information, when a command is requested from the diagnostic equipment). If the data writing process does not end normally, the PGM-FI ECU judges that the EEPROM is faulty and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch

blocks.text: Failure detection is performed for each EEPROM data write/read process, and if the data is not processed normally even once.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
